summary: Small single-storey rectangular house with a stepped brick gable roof, light-coloured stone trim and a raised foundation area; walls have a lower wood band, a middle sandstone band with long horizontal windows, and corner stone pillars. A white stone (likely quartz) low perimeter border surrounds the building on a dirt plot.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with a central pitched/stepped gable roof
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the roof ridge (front-back)

elements: long horizontal windows on each side (three window panes visible on front side)
central stepped brick gable roof with stone slab edging
white stone perimeter border (low wall) around building
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular footprint roughly 11x7 blocks; raise a 1-block high wooden skirting around the base, then a 3-block high sandstone band with 3-wide horizontal window openings on longer sides.
Place quartz (or white) blocks as a continuous trim band above the windows and as a low perimeter border 1 block away from the building edge.
Construct a stepped brick gable roof centered on the long axis: brick slabs/blocks in successive smaller layers with stone slabs at the exposed step edges to match the cap detail.
Use darker stone bricks or stonebrick blocks at the four vertical corners spanning from base to trim to reproduce corner pillars.
uncertainties: Exact block types for corner pillars: appear as darker stone (stonebrick or polished andesite) — identified as stonebrick with moderate confidence.
The white trim material might be quartz blocks or smooth sandstone/white concrete; quartz chosen with high but not absolute confidence.
Precise internal floor height and presence of interior details are not visible from provided views.
Exact footprint dimensions estimated from pixel views; actual block counts may differ by ±1-2 blocks in each axis.
